raw_value: This was so hard! It would be more helpful if we could see all the shapes or something before starting. Some shapes felt very similar to one another.

It wasn't really clear to me if the points for different mushrooms were consistent across the levels. It appears they were. I tried to focus on independent elements, like color, shape and stem width. Unfortunately, this wasn't particularly useful, as it appeared none of these were determinant. Instead, it was the combinations of the three that were important. The exception seemed to be that all red mushrooms had a positive value. All pink/fuchsia may have had negative value, but it was unclear. Because I wasn't able to figure out a way to isolate the individual elements, I found it rather difficult. With the exception of the red ones. On a few levels I tried to just keep going collecting reds. But, even when I played a single level for a long time it did not seem like it progressed it much further than levels where I hit "P" as soon as I was permitted to move forward. I don't want to sound like I was complaining when I said it was unenjoyable, but the difficulty for me definitely frustrated me. When I don't feel like I'm doing well at something, it motivates me to try harder. I suppose, if a monetary award was connected to how well I did, I probably would've created an excel sheet or something and taken detailed notes instead of trying to go just off of memory.
strstegy was seeing what the mushroom either added and or subtracted when jumping on boxes
i tried to determine a pattern, but was unsuccessful

there was no strategy to employ: you were fairly [----], as far as not really allowing me the opportunity to get a solid bead on what was "what?" in the kingdomnnall that i could cotton onto was, the reds were fairly consistent in not screwing me over
I went with random patters, that could start and stop each time I was selecting a mushroom that impacted me negatively

I locked in on a color that I knew to be rewarding, even if not very highly rewarding, but that I knew was basically always going to give stamina rather than take it away, and just farm the level that had those mushrooms in it.
I tried learning which colors and shaped had negative value and try to avoid them as much as I could
Tried to mentally assign scores to mushrooms based on the level type, then based on any past experience with a similiarly shaped mushroom.
I tried to memorize the mushrooms' thickness and cap size. I tried to remember which ones were very rewarding, how they looked, and what I often got from them.
Tried to remember the shape and colors that were good
I thought that the mushroom points were dependent on the specific arena, but I think that was the wrong strategy.
I tried color shapes , skipping boxes
I looked at the shape and width of the stems after I saw that wide caps and slender stems seemed to subtract.
I tried to avoid the color that frequently cause me to lose points.

Kept track of the width of the stem relative to the roundness and the color of the mushroom to get a good sense of what was poisonous or not.  Paid attention to certain combinations between those three characteristics.
I just tried to see which stems and tops combination got the most points and tried to remember them
Tried to rely on memory or rapid testing. Had ideas about certain colors in certain rooms. Trial and error.
I just began picking random mushrooms.
I just went after all the mushrooms til i noticed a pattern and then stuck to the pattern
trying to remember which mushrooms awared the most positive energy points and which had negative point
I TRIED TO REMEMBER HOW MANY POINTS EACH COLOR GAVE ME AND I TRIED TO MEMORIZE THE SHAPE OF THE MUSHROOM TO GET THE BEST ONE AND IT WORKED OUT MOST OF THE TIME, I RAELLY LIKED THE SURVEY THANKS.
trying to only eat the flatter caps with wider stems, always eat read, eat most white, some blue.
I tried to identify at least 2 colors that were safe each round and 1 that wasn't. But sometimes it changed and I just kinda went with it.
Identify commonly rewarded colors, then identify which area they spawn in. From there, eat everything.

I had to memorize certain mushrooms by a general concept, such as 'x color mushroom is often good in any variety for this color, or that this x color mushroom is too skinny or flat and must be avoided'. nnMostly the caps color was to be examined and memorized, however the stem color was only memorized if it was visually extreme, such as extremely large stem, or extremely skinny stem. nnCertain colors such as blue might have been avoided altogether unless it was irregular (too skinny, too flat) looking. Certain colors such as green or cyan was generally favored since the rng in what was given to me (most normal looking mushrooms, normal cap and normal stem) was generally given a positive bonus rather than a negative one and was chosen more often. nnCertain mushrooms however such as magenta or black was not seem too often, so it was guesswork based on a few mushrooms that I did see.
i checked for the width and most rewarding mushroom
My strategy was to take a mental note of the characteristics of the mushrooms.
